name: Milk Chocolate Praline
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine sugar, water, corn syrup, vinegar, and salt.
2. Bring mixture to a full boil, stirring constantly.
3. Continue boiling without stirring for 3 minutes.
4. Remove from heat; let cool for 5 minutes.
5. Add chocolate morsels; stir quickly until melted.
6. Stir in pecans.
7. Quickly drop by measuring tablespoonfuls onto foil-lined cookie sheets (work as rapidly as possible, as the mixture tends to set up quickly).
8. Garnish with pecan halves, if desired.
9. Refrigerate until set, about 20 minutes.
10. Peel candies off to serve. Store in refrigerator.
Makes 4 dozen 2-inch candies. Toll House Heritage Cookbook, Rev. 1984. From Tony Balano.
